question: Will the ECB raise its deposit facility rate at its July 2026 monetary policy meeting?

factors.description: Market pricing implies ~80%+ hold, 15-20% July hike
Analyst surveys more often place the next hike at September, not July
Hawkish members (Wunsch) call July 'in play'; ~3% 2026 inflation keeps a hike a live tail
ECB just hiked in June and adopted neutral, meeting-by-meeting communication avoiding signaling a series of hikes; back-to-back hikes would be aggressive escalation

summary: The ECB deposit rate stands at 2.25% (effective June 17, 2026), confirmed by FRED. For the July 23 meeting, market pricing points to a hold as the clear baseline (~80%+) with a 15-20% chance of a 25bp hike; analyst surveys more often flag September for the next move. The ECB has emphasized data-dependence and neutral communication after June's hike, and consecutive back-to-back hikes would be an aggressive escalation the Council has signaled it wants to avoid. A hawkish inflation backdrop (~3% in 2026) and vocal minority keep the hike a live tail but not the base case. I settle at the low end of the market range at ~13%.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 13% YES**

The ECB deposit rate is at 2.25% (effective June 17, 2026, confirmed via FRED). For the July 23 meeting, market pricing places a hold as the clear baseline (~80%+) with only a 15-20% chance of a further 25bp hike, and analyst surveys more often point to September for the next move. After June's hike, the Council adopted neutral, meeting-by-meeting communication explicitly avoiding signals of a hike series — back-to-back hikes would be an aggressive escalation. The ~3% 2026 inflation backdrop and hawkish members like Wunsch keep a hike a live tail, but not the base case. Final probability: **0.13**.
